subject: [PATCH v4 8/8] mm: switch deferred split shrinker to list_lru

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner
reviews.summary:
reviewer noted that deferred_split_folio() takes the lock via list_lru_lock_irqsave() with the original folio_memcg(), but set_shrinker_bit() uses this memcg even if it's dying or reparented, leading to incorrect shrinker bit reporting

reviewer noted that deferred_split_folio() takes the lock via list_lru_lock_irqsave() with the original folio's memcg, which may be dying and reparented, leading to a wrong memcg being used in __list_lru_add(), and suggested setting result to SCAN_ALLOC_HUGE_PAGE_FAIL in this case
reviews.raw_body:
I think you're right. Good catch.

This looks like an existing list_lru issue, actually. Even before,
list_lru_add() would call lock_list_lru_of_memcg() which might do the
hierarchy walk. But then set_shrinker_bit() on the passed in memcg.

I'll fix this in list_lru first. It's a likely backport
candidate. Then rebase my patches on top.

---

Good point. Sashiko pointed that out as well. I have a follow-up fix.

Thanks for taking a look.

2026-05-26.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug in the deferred split shrinker where it incorrectly sets the shrinker bit on the wrong memory cgroup (memcg) when reparenting occurs. The issue arises from using the original memcg of the folio, which may be dying and already reparented, instead of the parent memcg that owns the locked sublist. To fix this, the patch suggests setting the result to SCAN_ALLOC_HUGE_PAGE_FAIL in case of allocation failure.
